title: Escalating costs and military risks threaten Middle East truce between US and Iran
summary: Reports indicate the cost of the conflict has exceeded $29 billion as a fragile truce faces collapse. While Iran has issued conditions for renewed negotiations, the US administration suggests economic pain is secondary to stopping Iran's nuclear program, and the UAE has reportedly launched a secret attack that risks drawing more Gulf states into the war.

title: Ukraine and Russia exchange long-range drone strikes on energy and military infrastructure
summary: Ukraine launched deep strikes against Russian gas facilities in the Orenburg region and Bashkortostan, while Russia targeted Kryvyi Rih and other Ukrainian cities with over 100 drones. Ukrainian officials report civilian casualties and damage to energy sites as both nations intensify their use of strategic aviation and long-range unmanned aerial vehicles.

title: Donald Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping
summary: The meeting in Beijing focuses on trade tensions and regional stability amid the ongoing conflict with Iran. International coverage highlights the shift in the bilateral relationship since their last meeting nearly nine years ago, with both sides seeking to project an appearance of stability.

title: Anti-corruption probe in Ukraine targets former presidential aide Andriy Yermak
summary: Ukrainian anti-corruption authorities have named Andriy Yermak and several others as suspects in a money laundering and corruption investigation involving millions of dollars. Prosecutors are seeking his arrest with a high bail amount, marking a significant escalation in domestic legal challenges for the administration.

title: Emmanuel Macron seeks renewed partnerships during Africa Forward summit in Kenya
summary: The French President met with African leaders in Nairobi to discuss immigration, regional conflicts in the DRC and Mali, and a move away from colonial-era ties. The summit featured major deal announcements and a speech by António Guterres highlighting African leadership, though Macron's onstage remarks drew some criticism.

title: Massive protests in Argentina against university funding cuts by Javier Milei
summary: Tens of thousands of students and citizens marched in Buenos Aires and other cities to defend public education. The demonstrations are a response to budget cuts implemented by the Milei administration, which protesters argue threaten the future of the country's higher education system.

title: Nunes Marques takes office as President of Brazil's Superior Electoral Court
summary: Kassio Nunes Marques has assumed the presidency of the TSE, emphasizing the defense of freedom of expression and the integrity of electronic voting machines. His appointment is closely watched by allies of Jair Bolsonaro, given his previous votes regarding the former president's eligibility.

title: Keir Starmer faces internal pressure and calls for resignation in the UK
summary: The British Prime Minister is resisting calls to step down as the Labour Party faces internal divisions over his leadership. Critics and former advisers suggest he should make way for new leadership, while supporters point to party rules that make ousting a sitting leader difficult.

title: Israel and Lebanon exchange fire as ceasefire efforts remain strained
summary: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in multiple casualties, including paramedics and soldiers, while Hezbollah continues drone operations in northern Israel. Despite the April 17 ceasefire, Israel has ordered new evacuations in several Lebanese border towns as bombardment intensifies.

title: South Korea and Australia consider joining naval missions in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: South Korea is reviewing phased contributions to a US-led freedom of navigation initiative, while Australia has announced it will join a mission led by the UK and France. The moves come as international pressure mounts to secure shipping lanes against blockades and maritime incidents linked to regional tensions.

title: Israel qualifies for Eurovision final amid protests and boycotts
summary: The Eurovision Song Contest has been overshadowed by the conflict in Gaza, leading to boycotts by several countries and protests during the live semifinals. Despite the controversy and the expulsion of some activists from the venue, Israel successfully moved forward to the final round.

title: Trump administration officials resign amid policy shifts and internal pressure
summary: The head of the FDA, Marty Makary, has stepped down following reported pressure to authorize flavored e-cigarettes, while new leadership has been appointed at ICE. These departures come as the administration faces legal challenges over border wall land seizures and requests for a $1.2 trillion 'Golden Dome' missile defense system.

title: Nigeria reports airstrike casualties and interception of Starlink devices
summary: Amnesty International reports that a Nigerian airstrike killed over 100 people at a market in Zamfara. Separately, military officials claim to have intercepted 400 Starlink devices allegedly intended for use by terrorist groups in the region.

summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman appeared in court to address allegations from Elon Musk, testifying that Musk sought 90% control of the company. The trial centers on claims that the organization's transition from a non-profit model constituted a breach of its original mission.

title: Global health authorities monitor hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
summary: The World Health Organization is tracking at least 11 cases of hantavirus, including a French patient in critical condition. While the WHO suggests it is unlikely to become a pandemic on the scale of COVID-19, officials are investigating whether the strain has mutated as cases appear in Spain and France.

title: US inflation rises to 3.8% driven by high energy costs
summary: Consumer prices in the United States reached a three-year high in April, largely due to the encarecimiento of gasoline linked to Middle East tensions. The data has led to market volatility and political debate over proposed suspensions of the gas tax.

title: NBA players Jason Collins and Brandon Clarke die on the same day
summary: The basketball community is mourning Jason Collins, the first openly gay player in the NBA, who died of a brain tumor at 47, and Memphis Grizzlies player Brandon Clarke, who passed away at age 29. Tributes have poured in from across the league for both athletes.
